finding: office_bridge is added to auto_attach_builtin_ids but deliberately excluded from is_builtin_server_id, so its mutating tools stay behind per-call approval per DEC-4.
All handlers are permission-gated: jsonrpc/list_documents on OfficeBridgeUse, get_settings on OfficeBridgeAdminRead, update_settings/connect on OfficeBridgeManage; migrations 132/133 grant correctly.
The JSON-RPC endpoint dispatches tools without re-checking the runtime office_bridge_settings.enabled toggle (only the chat-attach path checks it); defense-in-depth gap, not an escalation.

The extension is registered at order 29, colliding with citations' chat extension (also 29); the comment claiming 29 is a distinct/deterministic slot is false. Both run before MCP (30), so impact is nondeterministic relative ordering + a false invariant comment.
u16::try_from(settings.port).unwrap_or(44300) is safe (DB CHECK constrains port to 1..=65535); module order 97 is free; init gates on config kill-switch then host probe.
Serialized DTOs carry only enabled/port/timestamps/cert_fingerprint and readiness booleans — no bridge session token or secret leaks into responses.
Built-in MCP URL is pinned to loopback_host regardless of configured server.host; upsert stores no credentials.

JSON-RPC surface mirrors code_sandbox: notifications 202, initialize/tools-list/ping/tools-call 200, method-not-found/tool errors ride 200 as JSON-RPC errors, parse/invalid-request 400; protocolVersion matches the tree.

edit_document inputSchema marks text required for op=append_paragraph, but dispatch_tool reads a.text.unwrap_or_default(), silently appending an empty paragraph if a client omits text; schema/handler leniency mismatch.

Dispatch paths map failures to typed AppError->JsonRpcError with no unwrap/panic on external input; platform enumeration failures in list_documents are logged and downgraded to an empty list rather than 500.
get_settings uses fetch_one on the seeded singleton row; update_settings COALESCEs Nones; DB errors map to AppError (500), no panic. Singleton CHECK and idempotent grant/upsert are correct.

serve_taskpane mints+registers a fresh session token on every taskpane.html load but revoke() is never called outside tests, so SESSION_TOKENS grows unbounded for the process lifetime (memory leak plus an ever-widening set of forever-valid tokens).
serve_static copies embedded asset bytes and serve_taskpane does from_utf8_lossy+replace per GET; negligible for these small assets.

The CA is minted BasicConstraints::Unconstrained (no pathLen/name constraints/bounded validity) and pushed into the OS Root store, so any cert signed by that CA key would be machine-trusted for ANY host; mitigated only by the CA private key being discarded, which is fragile and undocumented.
post_sink enforces a session token but performs no Origin allowlist check, unlike the /bridge WSS upgrade which requires both, so a token-bearing cross-origin caller is accepted at the POST sinks.
WSS/POST token auth uses hashed constant-time per-entry compares, Origin is checked before upgrade, and the token rides Sec-WebSocket-Protocol not the URL — auth design is sound.

office_is_elevated() runs a blocking CreateToolhelp32Snapshot enumeration synchronously via a non-async trait method, so an async caller invoking it inline blocks a tokio worker (should be spawn_blocking like the other COM paths).

COM work is confined to spawn_blocking threads each with a per-thread STA via ComGuard (CoInitializeEx/CoUninitialize balanced, RPC_E_CHANGED_MODE handled); VARIANT/BSTR/interface refcounts managed by the windows crate with no double-free/leak.
run_elevated_certutil ignores WaitForSingleObject's return; on WAIT_TIMEOUT it deletes the temp .cer while elevated certutil may still read it, and the timeout is misreported as a nonzero exit code.
com_get_string swallows all COM errors to an empty string, so a document whose Name read fails is pushed with name="" rather than skipped or surfaced.

diff_open_docs keys identity on full_name, but the same doc can be enumerated with different full_name values (COM full path vs window_enum_presence title); a transient COM-attach failure on one poll flips identity and emits a spurious close+open pair.

Token injection replaces only the quoted "__ZIEE_BRIDGE_TOKEN__" literal; if the shipped placeholder is unquoted or differs, the served pane silently carries no token and its WSS connect fails with no server-side error.
The bad-Origin, missing-token and invalid-token cases do genuinely assert rejection (is_err after a valid connect succeeds), so the core rejection coverage is present.
The three rejection cases assert only is_err() and never check the HTTP status (403 bad Origin vs 401 bad token), so a rejection for the wrong reason (or a TLS error) would still pass green.

The spec mocks the live /api/office-bridge/documents endpoint plus SSE and /messages, so it verifies rendering of a hand-seeded payload only and cannot catch a real enumeration/serialization bug (mock shape does match the server's snake_case OfficeApp contract).

The comment claims the listener does a bare literal replace of __ZIEE_BRIDGE_TOKEN__; the server replaces only the QUOTED form, so a maintainer following the comment with a replace-all would clobber the window.__ZIEE_BRIDGE_TOKEN__ accessor and silently break token pickup.

Commands.Url / FunctionFile points at taskpane.html rather than a dedicated function-command page; with only ShowTaskpane controls this is schema filler, but the function runtime would load the full pane and open a second WSS connection.

The per-session token is stored in a global window.__ZIEE_BRIDGE_TOKEN__, readable by the third-party office.js loaded from appsforoffice.microsoft.com in the same origin (accepted add-in tradeoff; a CDN supply-chain compromise would expose the token).
All DOM writes use textContent, no innerHTML/eval sink, and the injected token is base64url inside a quoted JS string literal, so no XSS/injection surface is introduced.
refetch is self-gated via hasUsePermission() before any fetch, so sync:reconnect/sync:office_document never 403 for a user lacking office_bridge::use — the no-403 rule holds.

store subscribes in init via on('sync:office_document'|'sync:reconnect', reload) plus an eager self-gated load(); panel reads declaratively from Stores.OfficeBridge — no useX()/useEffect load anti-pattern.

load never passes onError to refetchOpenDocuments, so a fetch failure is silently swallowed and the panel (loading/empty/list but no error branch) degrades to the empty state with no user-visible error.
loading Spinner has a label, Empty a description, status conveyed by text-bearing Tags (not color-only), and the open button has a text accessible name — core a11y is sound.
decorative <FileText/> icons render without aria-hidden, so assistive tech may surface a nameless graphic alongside the adjacent label.
tool_result renderer uses priority 73 but a static contentMatch claiming only list_open_documents, so the registry's continue skips it for every other tool_result — no hijack.

module registers Stores.OfficeBridge via store-kit defineStore + declaration-merge types, dependencies ['chat'], and auto-discovered chat-extension — conforms to the Stores.X + init(ctx) architecture.
raw English copy and manual pluralization are untokenized, but this matches the repo-wide convention (no t()/i18next; data-testids for i18n-safety), so not a defect.

contentMatch claims the block only when block.name==='list_open_documents'; if the backend emits the tool result under a namespaced/prefixed name the card silently never renders (contract dependency not verifiable from the frontend diff).
panel data shape {documents} matches PanelRendererMap['office-bridge']; OpenDoc field access matches api-client types; updateRightPanelTab payload type-checks.
